title: The Wedding Of Latka And Simka

jokes.text: Latka greets multiple personalities by name: Hello, Arlene. Hello, Jose. Hello, Dwayne. Hello, Billy. Hello, Ruby.
jokes.setup: Latka greeting people in a normal way
jokes.punchline: These are all his multiple personalities he's saying goodbye to
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show his multiple personalities through formal goodbyes. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: A man is nothing without friends.
jokes.setup: Latka just said goodbye to multiple personalities
jokes.punchline: He calls his mental disorder symptoms 'friends'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one line that reframes his mental illness as friendship. Sweet and absurd.

jokes.text: It's probably a witch doctor. This guy goes to a therapist like... Look what you gave me... Three twos.
jokes.setup: Character skeptical about Latka's therapist, starting a comparison
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by bad poker hand mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection but not particularly memorable or character-specific.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Dr. Joyce Brothers.
jokes.setup: Latka brings his therapist to meet friends
jokes.punchline: It's actually the famous TV psychologist Dr. Joyce Brothers
jokes.explanation: Celebrity cameo that works because it's genuinely surprising and fits the show's absurd reality.

jokes.text: You helped me get through puberty.
jokes.setup: Tony recognizing Dr. Joyce Brothers
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about how her advice helped with puberty
jokes.explanation: Mild TMI humor that's believable for the character and situation.

jokes.text: Are you visiting the garage, or am I on The Merv Griffin show?
jokes.setup: Louie sees famous TV personality in his garage
jokes.punchline: Questions whether he's on TV instead of reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - cynical, self-aware, and economically structured. Very quotable.

jokes.text: 'Cause I have a story that Merv would've loved.
jokes.setup: Louie disappointed he's not on TV
jokes.punchline: Implies he has entertaining stories ready for talk shows
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's delusional confidence in his entertainment value.

jokes.text: the only thing that separates us from the animals are mindless superstition and pointless rituals.
jokes.setup: Latka explaining why his culture's wedding rituals matter
jokes.punchline: Says the distinguishing features are 'mindless' and 'pointless'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - defends traditions by calling them absurd. Highly quotable philosophical humor.

jokes.text: Usually it's an old hag or a leper or the village idiot. But you'll do in a pinch.
jokes.setup: Explaining who normally serves as Gewirtzal
jokes.punchline: Alex qualifies because he's equally undesirable
jokes.explanation: Classic insult structure with good escalation from terrible options to Alex as backup.

jokes.text: That's it? You're just going to grepse at me?
jokes.setup: Simka expecting some elaborate surprise from Latka
jokes.punchline: The surprise is just him making a foreign gesture/expression at her
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from made-up foreign word and anticlimatic surprise.

jokes.text: I'm just glad you're not a leper.
jokes.setup: Alex struggling with the foreign proposal ritual
jokes.punchline: Simka references earlier mention of lepers as typical Gewirtzals
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Simka's relief at getting a healthy Gewirtzal.

jokes.text: I am going to die a bachelor.
jokes.setup: Alex struggling to sing the proposal properly
jokes.punchline: Latka's dramatic conclusion that the bad singing dooms him to eternal bachelorhood
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific dramatic overreaction that fits Latka's anxious personality.

jokes.text: You... You have grabbed the nose of the Gewirtzal
jokes.setup: Alex gives heartfelt speech about Latka and Simka's love
jokes.punchline: Simka responds with bizarre foreign idiom for acceptance
jokes.explanation: Absurd foreign idiom that contrasts beautifully with Alex's sincere romantic speech.

jokes.text: Men are nothing but lazy lumps of drunken flesh.
jokes.setup: Simka asking to hear the traditional wisdom (murzik)
jokes.punchline: The 'wisdom' is brutally cynical about men
jokes.explanation: Shocking contrast between expected wedding wisdom and brutal cynicism. Very quotable.

jokes.text: They crowd you in bed, get you all worked up, and then before you can say, 'Is that all there is?' that's all there is.
jokes.setup: Already established the cynical tone about men
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual disappointment disguised as traditional wisdom
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed escalation with perfect pacing and highly quotable punchline structure.

jokes.text: your once firm breasts will fall to the floor.
jokes.setup: Continuing the brutal 'wisdom' about marriage
jokes.punchline: Absurdly extreme physical deterioration prediction
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that goes so extreme it becomes funny rather than just depressing.

jokes.text: Almost more than ever.
jokes.setup: After hearing brutal truths about marriage, asked if she still wants to marry
jokes.punchline: Says she wants it 'almost more than ever' - nearly unaffected
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that subverts expectation. The 'almost' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: You cannot walk on my son's furniture.
jokes.setup: Simka trying to kiss mother-in-law on cheek
jokes.punchline: Mother-in-law won't bend, forcing Simka to climb on furniture
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual situation created by rigid adherence to tradition creating impossible logistics.

jokes.text: Mothers-in-law are forbidden by tradition to bend.
jokes.setup: The physical impossibility of the kiss situation
jokes.punchline: Completely serious explanation of absurd traditional restriction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - taking tradition to literal extreme. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Be it ever so humble, there's no place like hell.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting that the place looks more cheerful
jokes.punchline: Louie twists the 'home sweet home' saying to call it hell
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie cynicism with a well-executed familiar saying twist.

jokes.text: A sense of humor can cushion you from the harsh realities of life. It can also make you seem like a real bummer.
jokes.setup: Dr. Brothers giving wise advice about humor
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with a cynical observation
jokes.explanation: Smart self-aware humor that reflects on the nature of comedy itself.

jokes.text: Don't take me seriously. I'm flirting with you.
jokes.setup: Dr. Brothers being unusually cynical
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's flirting with Louie, explaining the behavior
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from unlikely romantic pairing and direct admission.

jokes.text: they are lamenting the bride's future as slave to her husband and the groom's burden of family responsibility which will eventually crush him.
jokes.setup: Alex asking why the women look so unhappy at a wedding
jokes.punchline: The 'celebration' is actually lamenting future misery
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about cultural differences in viewing marriage.

jokes.text: Oh, I live in London where I work as a weatherman for the BBC.
jokes.setup: Alex asking about Mascha's accent
jokes.punchline: Random specific modern job completely disconnected from his cultural role here
jokes.explanation: Absurd juxtaposition between ancient cultural traditions and modern BBC employment.

jokes.text: What about 'What is the most unusual thing about our ceremony?' Well, that's a good one.
jokes.setup: Mascha asking if there are questions
jokes.punchline: He asks himself the perfect question and compliments his own question
jokes.explanation: Mild meta humor from character essentially interviewing himself.

jokes.text: To truly understand the feelings and emotions of their mate, the bride and groom must marry in each other's clothes.
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding ceremony explanation
jokes.punchline: Latka and Simka are wearing each other's wedding outfits
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag that's both funny and oddly touching with pseudo-meaningful explanation.

jokes.text: You don't know how long I have waited to see you in your father's wedding dress.
jokes.setup: Latka seeing Simka in cross-dressed wedding attire
jokes.punchline: He's specifically excited to see her in his father's dress
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that makes the absurd tradition feel romantic and personal.

jokes.text: Never have I felt a man's warm, moist lips pressing against my waiting mouth.
jokes.setup: Simka protesting her virginity after crown falls
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic description while claiming innocence
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - protesting innocence with increasingly graphic detail.

jokes.text: the air humid... Simka! Simka!
jokes.setup: Simka's increasingly passionate description of what she's never done
jokes.punchline: Latka desperately trying to stop her before she goes too far
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character dynamic with Latka's panicked interruption.

jokes.text: She said $5,100.
jokes.setup: Priest asks what's the greatest gift one person can give another
jokes.punchline: Simka's answer is translated as a specific dollar amount
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - turning philosophical question into specific monetary answer.

jokes.text: The kid. Yourself!
jokes.setup: Priest asks who to save - baby or Simka from wild boar
jokes.punchline: Latka chooses baby, Simka angrily corrects him
jokes.explanation: Perfect marital comedy - husband fails test by being selfless, wife corrects priorities.

jokes.text: That was the final test. One of them had to stand up and demand to be married.
jokes.setup: Wedding appears to be canceled due to failed test
jokes.punchline: The 'failure' was actually the secret final test they needed to pass
jokes.explanation: Satisfying story twist that reframes everything - the emotional moment was the real test.

tmdb_overview: Latka, who Joyce Brothers has declared is cured of his multiple personality disorder, asks Simka to marry him. They then must endure a bizarre series of tests, as is the marital tradition for members of their religion. In hilarious fashion, things get ugly at the ceremony itself, and Simka denounces her religion, when her minister reveals that she has just passed the last test and they are now married.
insight: Wedding chaos delivers Taxi's absurdist peak with 32 jokes in controlled escalation.
